reforms.description: New legislation promoting better structured and simpler SME support schemes, including for administrative procedures, entered into force in 2017 to enhance the innovative capacity of SMEs in Slovakia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the innovative capacity of SMEs is expected to raise productivity and contribute positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The legislation has entered into force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly improves the support framework for SMEs, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: New legislation promoting better structured and simpler SME support schemes, including for administrative procedures, entered into force in 2017.

reforms.description: The Slovak Republic has recognized the need to increase R&D spending, which currently stands at only around 1% of GDP, to enhance productivity and move up the global value chain.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D spending is essential for enhancing productivity and competitiveness in the global market.
reforms.status_evidence: The need to increase R&D spending has been recognized but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to boost public R&D funding, which is critical for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: There is substantial scope to raise the quality of university research... Along with more R&D... tertiary education changes could also move Slovakia up the global value chain and stimulate productivity growth.

reforms.description: Slovakia introduced financial incentives for adopting new technology and innovation spending, including the development of innovation vouchers and a reform of the R&D tax credit to make it refundable.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Financial incentives for innovation are expected to stimulate private sector investment in technology and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The financial incentives and reforms have been successfully introduced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the innovation support framework through financial incentives.
reforms.source_quote: On January 2015 a new tax relief, called 'superdeduction', was introduced, allowing private companies to deduct 125% of their expenses for R&D from their tax base.

reforms.description: Slovakia decided to launch three new international cooperation programmes linked to Horizon 2020, focusing on applied research and experimental development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: International cooperation in applied research is expected to enhance innovation and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes have been launched successfully.
reforms.importance_rationale: The launch of new cooperation programmes is significant for enhancing Slovakia's research capabilities.
reforms.source_quote: In 2016 it was decided to launch three new international cooperation programmes linked to Horizon 2020 European funding programme.

reforms.description: The Slovak government is discussing initiatives to ease immigration laws for non-EU skilled workers and enhance its strategy to attract international students by increasing the provision of tertiary education programs in foreign languages and strengthening scholarship programs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled workers and international students is crucial for enhancing the talent pool and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is currently discussing these initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: These initiatives are significant for improving the human capital landscape in Slovakia.
reforms.source_quote: The government should increase the provision of tertiary education programmes in foreign languages and strengthen scholarship programmes for talented foreign students.

reforms.description: The Slovak government has launched a reform to support more work-based learning through a dual apprenticeship system, enhancing collaboration between employers and vocational schools.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing work-based learning is expected to improve the skills of the workforce and support innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The dual apprenticeship system has been successfully launched.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the vocational education system, which is crucial for building human capital.
reforms.source_quote: In 2015 the National Council approved the New Act on Vocational Education and Training, which introduces the provision of VET in a dual system based on collaboration between employers and vocational schools.

reforms.description: The Slovak government has implemented a 'Return home' scheme to attract skilled emigrants back to the country, providing financial incentives for top university graduates to work in the public sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled emigrants is expected to enhance the talent pool and support innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This scheme is significant for improving the human capital landscape in Slovakia.
reforms.source_quote: Since 2015 the 'Return home' scheme with a budget of 1 million euros has been providing subsidies of 10000 or 50000 euros for top university graduates.

reforms.description: The recent creation of a health technology assessment agency by the Ministry of Health aims to analyze the clinical and cost-effectiveness of new health technologies and drugs, promoting a more transparent and independent system for assessing performance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishing a health technology assessment agency is expected to improve the evaluation of health technologies, enhancing innovation in the health sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The agency has been created and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving health technology evaluation processes.
reforms.source_quote: The recent creation of a health technology assessment agency by the Ministry of Health to analyse clinical and cost effectiveness and safety of new health technologies and drugs is a positive step.
